libraryDescription:
This library, math.GaussianFunction, provides Gaussian functions.
Two function names are provided, gauss and gaussian, and the difference between them is the coefficient in the denominator of the exponent part.
The former function, gauss(x,a,b,c), calculates the value of a * exp( -(x-b)**2 / c**2 ).
The latter function, gaussian(x,a,b,c), calculates the value of a * exp( -(x-b)**2 / (2 * c**2) ). Note that the denominator of the exponent part has the coefficient 2.

This module also provides arbitrary-precision functions such as those for the varfloat and varcomplex types.
However, only the algorithms themselves are implemented, and no special measures are taken against the accumulation of calculation errors or loss of significance.

In other words, the returned value is not guaranteed to be correct to the last digit,and the tail of the result always contains an incorrect part caused by accumulated errors.
The length of this erroneous part varies depending on the algorithm used,the convergence speed for the given arguments, the degree of loss of significance, and other conditions.
For example, convergence generally becomes significantly slower near singular points, and accumulated errors also increase accordingly.

Therefore, when using arbitrary-precision functions such as those for the varfloat and varcomplex types,
call the function with a sufficiently larger number of digits than actually required,and discard the unnecessary part containing errors from the obtained result.

function0.name: gauss
function0.declaration: float gauss(float x, float a, float b, float c)
function0.description: The Gaussian function for real numbers.Returns the value of a * exp( -(x-b)**2 / c**2 ). The difference from the gaussian function is that the denominator of the exponent part does not have the factor 2.
function0.parameters:
(float type) x : The coordinate variable. 
(float type) a : The coefficient multiplied by the exponent part. 
(float type) b : The offset value of the coordinate. 
(float type) c : The square root of the denominator term in the exponent part. 
d : The linear coefficient of the denominator term in the exponent part.
function0.returnValue: (float type) The value of the exponential function.
